svg.text: Custom Variables, Node Graph Variables, Local Variables
Custom variables are data structures stored in an entity's custom variable component. Creators can
define and use them. Common uses: recording player scores, hit counts, mechanism states, etc.
Custom variable names (string type). Cannot share the same name within the same entity.
Operations: reading, modifying, and monitoring events all rely on custom variable names.
Prefab ID List / Config ID List
Out-of-range input returns last valid value
Follows the component (and entity). When entity
is destroyed, custom variables are destroyed too.
Cannot be accessed or modified after destruction.
Global read/write: any entity can read and modify
custom variables on any other entity.
Events only pushed to the entity where node graph is mounted.
The custom variable component is the data carrier. Supports configuring multiple variables simultaneously.
- Custom variable name: unique identifier, no duplicates within same component
- Data type: strong typing, all variables must have types explicitly defined
- Default value: variable created with this value when entity is created
Variables do NOT need to be defined before use. "Set Custom Variable" node can dynamically create new variables.
Only default-configured variables are synchronized locally for Interface and local filters.
Components tab > Find custom variable component
(default for all units) or Add Common Components.
View count of defined variables on Components tab.
Predefined Variables: view/modify names, types, defaults
Add Custom Variable: add single or multiple variables
Stage Variables Overview: view all on stage entities
Filter by type; Batch Delete mode available
Warning: Modifying name or type of predefined variables, or batch deleting, may cause related
custom variable nodes in the node graph to fail to run because they cannot find the corresponding variable.
Creates new variable if name not found. Supports generic input.
Output: Event Source Entity, Event Source GUID,
Variable Name, Pre-Change Value, Post-Change Value
Triggered even if value unchanged. Filter by variable name recommended.
Query node. Same parameters as server version. Read-only from local node graph.
Only variables configured by default in the component are synchronized locally.
VIII. Container Types and Pass by Reference
All List, Dictionary, and Structure data types.
Get outputs a reference. Modifications directly
change the stored custom variable value.
Does NOT trigger "When Custom Variable Changes".
Use Get Custom Variable to obtain updated value.
Basic data types (Integer, Float, String, etc.).
Get outputs the value itself. Modifying this value
does NOT affect the stored custom variable.
Only Set Custom Variable node can trigger the
For container types, the "When Custom Variable Changes" event Pre-Change Value and Post-Change Value
outputs do NOT have values when triggered by Set Custom Variable. Use Get Custom Variable for updated value.
Life Cycle follows the Node Graph. Only valid in Server Node Graphs. Functionality similar to Custom
Variables but with different life cycle and scope. Can only be accessed within the Node Graph it resides in.
Names must be unique within same Node Graph. Multiple Node Graphs on same entity can share variable names.
Comparison: Node Graph Variables vs Custom Variables
All Basic and List Data Types
All Basic and List Data Types
X. When to Use Each Variable Type
Store general-purpose data belonging to an Entity.
Globally accessible. E.g., hit count of a mechanism
that other Entities/Node Graphs may access.
Store local, temporary variables belonging to the
Node Graph. E.g., temporary Player List in a function.
Enables modular design, fully within sandbox.
XI. Exposing Node Graph Variables to Stages
Click the Expose button beside a Node Graph Variable and save to expose it to the Stage layer.
In the Stage's Node Graph, view exposed variable count. Click Edit Variable Count to adjust overwrite
values per entity. E.g., different DMG values for different Explosive Barrel entities from same prefab.
Node Graph Editing Tab > Shortcut menu > Node Graph Variables tab. Drag variables into editing area to create Set/Get nodes.
